$source: W3C ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/) and axe-core rule descriptions (https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/). Spot-verified for accuracy 2026-07-01.

button-name.guidance: Every button must have an accessible name, provided by inner text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.

link-name.guidance: Every link must have discernible text — inner text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Icon-only links must supply an aria-label.

label.wcag_ref: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, SC 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value
label.guidance: Form controls must have an associated <label> (via for/id) or aria-label / aria-labelledby. Placeholder is not a label.

aria-allowed-attr.guidance: Only ARIA attributes allowed for the element's role may be used. Remove or replace disallowed attributes; do not invent new ones.

aria-required-attr.guidance: Add the ARIA attributes required by the element's role (e.g., role=checkbox requires aria-checked).

aria-valid-attr-value.guidance: ARIA attribute values must match the allowed token / type. Correct the value; do not invent a new one.

landmark-one-main.wcag_ref: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, SC 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks
landmark-one-main.guidance: Each page must have exactly one <main> element (or role="main") wrapping the primary content.

region.guidance: All content should be contained by a landmark (header, nav, main, footer, aside, section with accessible name, or their ARIA role equivalents).

duplicate-id-aria.wcag_ref: WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.1 Parsing (obsolete in 2.2) / SC 1.3.1 Info and Relationships
duplicate-id-aria.guidance: IDs referenced by aria-labelledby / aria-describedby / aria-controls must be unique. Rename or dedupe.

html-has-lang.guidance: The root <html> element must declare a lang attribute matching the primary document language.

document-title.guidance: Every page must have a non-empty <title> that describes the page's purpose.

frame-title.guidance: Every <iframe> must have a title attribute describing its content.
